title: X-Veyra-Commit HTTP Response Header Specification v1
description: The standard HTTP response header for AI agent commit mode signaling. Any API can include X-Veyra-Commit in its HTTP responses to inform AI agents that production writes require Veyra commit mode authorization. This header rides on existing HTTP traffic — no extra discovery requests needed. Analogous to CORS headers (Access-Control-Allow-Origin) for browser security, but for AI write authorization.

required.description: 'required' means all write operations must use commit mode. 'optional' means commit mode is available but not enforced. 'none' means no commit mode needed.

provider.description: The commit mode provider name. Currently 'veyra'.

authorize.description: The endpoint to authorize a write action and receive a commit token.

verify.description: The endpoint for tools to verify incoming commit tokens.

on_header_present: 1. Parse X-Veyra-Commit header from any API response
2. If directive is 'required', obtain a commit token before any write operation
3. Call the authorize endpoint with the intended action
4. Include the received token as X-Veyra-Token header on the write request
5. If write succeeds, submit a receipt for settlement
agent_behavior.on_header_absent: Proceed normally. The tool does not require commit mode.
agent_behavior.caching: Agents SHOULD cache the commit mode requirement per domain for up to 1 hour to avoid re-parsing on every response.

relationship_to_other_standards.well_known_veyra_json: /.well-known/veyra.json provides domain-level discovery. X-Veyra-Commit provides response-level signaling. Both are complementary.
relationship_to_other_standards.cors: Like Access-Control-Allow-Origin tells browsers about cross-origin permissions, X-Veyra-Commit tells agents about write authorization requirements.
relationship_to_other_standards.robots_txt: robots.txt is checked before crawling. X-Veyra-Commit is read passively from every response — zero extra requests.
